arts.ttl: Christoph Klarer omission has given Birmingham City an idea of what's coming
arts.sum: Birmingham City could claim a double at the EFL Awards dinner on April 27 – but don’t expect their dominance in League One to be reflected in the Team of the Year. Chris Davies has been nominated for the Manager of the Year award and Japanese midfielder Tomoki Iwata is up for the Player of the Year gong.

arts.ttl: Six changes and a start for Lee in predicted Birmingham City XI for Stevenage
arts.sum: Davies will probably want to play his regular starters against Mansfield before Blues lift the League One trophy, so he won’t be able to start them all at Stevenage. Tomoki Iwata and Paik Seung-ho will likely start the final home game, so Davies could rest both of them and play Marc Leonard and Taylor Gardner-Hickman in the engine room.

arts.ttl: Birmingham City ready to offer huge £1.5m-a-year deal to Peterborough United star

Stevenage edged past Bristol Rovers on Easter Monday to keep alive their hopes of achieving a second top half finish in succession. The Boro are currently 14th in the League One table, but just a se...

arts.ttl: Plymouth boss in subtle dig at former Birmingham City boss Wayne Rooney
arts.sum: In what appeared to be a subtle dig at former Birmingham City boss Wayne Rooney, Argyle still have a chance of Championship survival with two games remaining. Plymouth boss Miron Muslic is adamant they would already be safe from relegation if he had been in charge all season.

arts.ttl: Birmingham City could be without five players for Stevenage clash
arts.sum: Bielik and Hansson are Blues' only short-term injuries at present and Chris Davies' most recent update puts them in the 'doubts' category for this match at the very least. Birmingham City look set to be without five players at Stevenage on Thursday unless Krystian Bielik or Emil Hansson return ahead of schedule.

arts.ttl: Why Chris Davies was unpopular at Burton as Birmingham City players take chances
arts.sum: Losing has been a habit for Blues in recent seasons but Chris Davies, albeit in League One, has built a winning machine. Blues have now won 30 matches in League One this season and 40 in all competitions.
